messages.content: My husband told me last Tuesday that he wants a divorce. We have a 7-year-old daughter. I don't know what to do.
I'm sorry. That's a lot to take in, especially with your daughter involved. Can you tell me — do you own property together, or have joint financial accounts?
Yes, we have a flat in Pune. We bought it together 6 years ago. And we have joint savings.
Understood. And is there a family business or professional practice either of you own — fully or partly?
That simplifies things meaningfully. How would you describe the urgency — is there a timeline pressure, or do you have time to work through this carefully?
He wants to move out by end of month. So I feel some pressure but I want to do this right for my daughter.
That's a reasonable position and it's clear you're thinking of her first. One more thing — how long have you been married?
Thank you for sharing all of this. I have enough to start building your case. I'm assigning your team now.

extracted_case_profile.property_description: Joint flat in Pune — 6 years old
extracted_case_profile.additional_notes: Husband initiating. Daughter 7 years. Both salaried.
